title: Unidentified Killik Sith warrior

A [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] '''individual''' resided on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] long prior to [[27,000 BBY/Legends|27,000 BBY]]. Fleeing persecution, he fled to the world [[Sarafur]], where he came to reside in a [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb|building]] used by a [[Unidentified Sarafur species|native species]] for bathing in [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfurous]] [[mineral water]]s. After the Killik's [[Death/Legends|death]], his abode became his [[Burial/Legends|tomb]] and a site strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. At one point, the Alsakani [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]] [[Jilst Bindalin]] stored a stolen artifact in the Sith's tomb, and the structure was later explored by a group of [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunters]].

==Biography==
===A refugee in a doomed city===
[[File:Killik-TUR.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Sith warrior was a member of the Killik species (a Killik pictured).]]
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Killik/Legends|Killik]]<ref name="Tessent" /> was a member of a [[Hive mind/Legends|hive-minded]], [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> that [[Homeworld/Legends|originated]] on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. The Killiks were transplanted off their homeworld by the powerful [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]], and ultimately both species disappeared from the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[30,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive Killik resided on the Core Worlds planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]<ref name="Tessent" /> at some point long before [[27,000 BBY/Legends|27,000 BBY]].<ref name="SithDate" /> He was a member of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tradition of Force-users,<ref name="Tessent" /> which originally arose among the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban's]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] species—whom the Killiks attempted to [[Incursion of Korriban|conquer]] around 30,000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

At some point long before Alsakan was [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] by [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the Killik Sith warrior faced persecution on the planet. He fled to the<ref name="Tessent" /> Outer Rim<ref name="TEA" /> world [[Sarafur]],<ref name="Tessent" /> which at one point served as a Killik colony.<ref name="TEA" /> The Sith took up refuge in a [[City/Legends|city]] of a [[Unidentified Sarafur species|native species]] there, coming to reside in a [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb|building]] used by the natives to bathe in [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfurous]] [[mineral water]]s. Eventually, eons before the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="Tessent" /> which [[Declaration of a New Order|began]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> the Sarafur's native species went extinct, and at some point the Killik Sith warrior [[Death/Legends|died]], with the building he used to reside in becoming his [[Burial/Legends|tomb]]. The structure eventually became buried under tons of collapsed walls and columns.<ref name="Tessent" />

===Legacy===
After the Killik Sith's death, his tomb became a beacon in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The structure became inhabited by dark side [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]], and both individuals attuned to the Force and those who were not were affected by a dark and sinister presence. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the existence of the Empire, a legend told of a lone Sith guardian who was waiting to unleash his wrath upon any trespassers of his abode in the ruins of his adopted people. In addition, an old [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]] tale recounted the Killik Sith warrior's flight from Alsakan to Sarafur. The Alsakani [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]] [[Jilst Bindalin]] at one point learned of the latter legend and located the Sith's tomb. After stealing an artifact, Bindalin took it to Sarafur. He stored it in the Killik's tomb as a form of petty vengeance and was subsequently never seen again.<ref name="Tessent" />

{{RPG|"[[Race for the Tessent]]"}}
At some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="3 ABY">By default, the events of {{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}} are set during the opposition to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars (1977) 69|''Star Wars'' (1977) 69]], which depict the [[Battle of the City of Bone|end]] of the Imperial presence on Mandalore per {{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}, to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> a group of [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunters]] arrived on Sarafur during a search for the artifacts known as the [[Tessent]]s. The group explored the Killik Sith's tomb, discovering a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] there.<ref name="Tessent" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Race_for_the_Tessent_G9.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Killik Sith warrior was introduced in the roleplaying adventure "Race for the Tessent."]]
The Killik Sith warrior was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Race for the Tessent]]," which was published for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Tessent" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The adventure describes the Sith as an "artisan" but does not provide elaboration for the choice of placing that descriptor in quotation marks.<ref name="Tessent" />

Instead of featuring a set plot, "Race for the Tessent" presents several settings in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]], Sarafur being one of them, that the player characters may visit during their search for one of the Tessent artifacts. For each of the settings, the adventure also suggests possible circumstances for a Tessent being hidden there, with the section dedicated to Sarafur noting how at some point Jilst Bindalin could have stolen the [[Alsakan Tessent]] and brought it to the Killik Sith warrior's tomb on the planet. This article assumes that as much of the information presented in the adventure as possible without contradictions is [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity and therefore assumes that the Alsakan Tessent was instead hidden on the planet [[Ast Kikorie/Legends|Ast Kikorie]].<ref name="Tessent" />

Additionally, "Race for the Tessent" appears to give contradictory information regarding the dating of the Killik's flight from Alsakan. The adventure establishes that the Sith left the planet long before Humans had colonized it, yet it also states that Humans drove the Killik off Alsakan. In line with the assumptions this article makes that are outlined above, it is also assumed both that the latter statement is in error and that the Sith's tomb was inhabited by dark side spirits instead of being deserted.<ref name="Tessent" />
